rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Judgment Based On Extrinsic Fraud; Request To Declare Legal Recovery Llc And Its Agent Demas Yan As Vexatious Litigants; Request For Judicial Notice Of Chuek Yan'S Debt Discharged In Bankruptcy Court Prior To Litigation; Declaration Of Reshma Kamath; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 29, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Judgment Based On Extrinsic Fraud; Request To Declare Legal Recovery Llc And Its Agent Demas Yan As Vexatious Litigants; Request For Judicial Notice Of Chuek Yan'S Debt Discharged In Bankruptcy Court Prior To Litigation; Declaration Of Reshma Kamath; Proof Of Service is DENIED in part and moot in part. Motion is moot as to the request for judicial notice. This relief was granted by January 19, 2024 ex-parte order. Motion is otherwise denied. Moving party failed to carry its burden regarding extrinsic fraud and vexations litigant provisions. Additional arguments and requests for relief brought up for the first time in reply are not considered. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE CHOW VS. TRI COUNTIES BANK, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Defendant Tri Counties Bank To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 29, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT TRI COUNTIES BANK Notice Of Petition And Petition Of Defendant Tri Counties Bank To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is GRANTED. This action shall proceed in arbitration. Upon completion of the arbitration, parties shall proceed in Court on the equitable relief available under the causes of action for wrongful foreclosure and UCL violations if such relief is warranted based on the arbitration outcome. Parties' contract contains a valid binding arbitration provision that has not been waived and is not unconscionable. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARY PHAM VS. SVETLANA KONOVICH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 29, 2024 line . DEFENDANT SVETLANA KONOVICH DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is continued to February 7, 2024 for Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief providing authorities for the proposition that Plaintiff's demand for rent for the periods subsequent to the events giving rise to the termination of the lease is consistent with Plaintiff's position that the lease is terminated and the breach is not waived. Supplemental brief to be filed and served no later than February 2, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
